content:
# 1 Peter Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), pages 265-266 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1PE_001_page_001.jpg, 1PE_001_page_002.jpg

1. Petros, an apostle of Yeshua Christos, to [the] chosen sojourners of [the] dispersion of Pontos, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

2. according to [the] foreknowledge of God [the] Father, in sanctification of [the] Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling of [the] blood of Yeshua Christos: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.

3. Blessed [be] the God and Father of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos, the [one] according to the great mercy of him having begotten us again into a living hope through [the] resurrection of Yeshua Christos from [the] dead,

4. into an inheritance incorruptible and undefiled and unfading, having been kept in [the] heavens for you,

5. the [ones] being guarded by [the] power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in [the] last time,

6. in which you rejoice greatly, [though] now for a little [while], if it is necessary, having been grieved by various trials,

7. that the testing of the faith of you — much more precious than gold, the [thing] perishing, yet being tested through fire — might be found for praise and glory and honor at [the] revelation of Yeshua Christos,

8. whom not having seen, you love; in whom now not seeing, yet believing, you rejoice with joy inexpressible and having been glorified,

9. receiving the end of the faith of you, [the] salvation of souls.

10. Concerning which salvation [the] prophets sought out and searched out, the [ones] having prophesied concerning the grace [intended] for you,

11. searching into what or what manner of time the Spirit of Christos in them was making clear, testifying beforehand the sufferings for Christos and the glories after these [things].

12. To whom it was revealed that not to themselves but to you they were ministering these [things], which now were announced to you through the [ones] having proclaimed the good news to you by [the] Holy Spirit having been sent from heaven — into which [things] angels desire to look.

13. Therefore, having girded up the loins of the mind of you, being sober, hope completely upon the grace being brought to you at [the] revelation of Yeshua Christos.

14. As children of obedience, not being conformed to the former desires [of you] in the ignorance of you,

15. but according to the Holy [One] having called you, you also yourselves become holy in all [your] conduct,

16. because it has been written, "Holy you shall be, because I [am] holy."

17. And if you call upon [the] Father, the [one] judging impartially according to the work of each, conduct [yourselves] in fear [during] the time of the sojourning of you,

18. knowing that not by corruptible [things] — silver or gold — were you redeemed from the futile conduct handed down from [the] fathers of you,

19. but by precious blood, as of a lamb unblemished and spotless, [the blood] of Christos,

20. having been foreknown indeed before [the] foundation of [the] world, but having been manifested at [the] last of the times for the sake of you,

21. the [ones] through him believing in God, the [one] having raised him from [the] dead and having given glory to him, so that the faith of you and hope are in God.

22. Having purified the souls of you in the obedience of the truth for unhypocritical brotherly love, from [a] pure heart love one another earnestly,

23. having been begotten again not from corruptible seed but incorruptible, through [the] word of God living and remaining,

24. because "All flesh [is] as grass, and all [the] glory of it as [the] flower of grass. The grass withered and the flower fell off,

25. but the word of [the] Lord remains into the age." And this is the word, the [one] having been proclaimed as good news to you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 -- "chosen sojourners" (eklektois parepidemois):** Sinaiticus reads eklektois parepidemois diasporas -- "chosen sojourners of [the] dispersion." The term parepidemois ("resident aliens, sojourners") emphasizes the temporary, pilgrim status of believers in the world. The five provinces listed trace a route through Roman Asia Minor.

2. **v.2 -- "in sanctification of [the] Spirit" (en hagiasmo pneumatos):** Sinaiticus preserves the Trinitarian structure of the greeting: the Father's foreknowledge, the Spirit's sanctification, the Son's blood. This three-fold pattern frames the entire letter.

3. **v.3 -- "having begotten us again" (anagennesas):** Sinaiticus reads anagennesas hemas -- the verb anagennao ("beget again, regenerate") appears in the NT only here and at 1 Pet 1:23. This is distinctly Petrine vocabulary not found in Paul.

4. **v.7 -- "the testing of the faith of you" (to dokimion):** The word dokimion means the "tested portion" or "genuine part" of faith -- that which survives the refining process. The gold comparison is explicit: faith's testing is more valuable than the testing of gold, which itself perishes despite being refined.

5. **v.8 -- "having been glorified" (dedoxasmene):** Sinaiticus reads the perfect passive participle dedoxasmene modifying "joy" (chara) -- the joy is already glorified, a present participation in future glory. Some witnesses read aneklaleto ("unspeakable") alone; Sinaiticus preserves the fuller reading.

6. **v.11 -- "the Spirit of Christos" (to pneuma Christou):** Sinaiticus reads "the Spirit of Christ in them" -- a remarkable claim that the pre-existent Christ's Spirit was operative in the OT prophets. The prophets searched their own Spirit-given revelations to understand the timing.

7. **v.16 -- "Holy you shall be" (hagioi genesthe/esesthe):** Sinaiticus reads the imperative/future form, quoting Lev 11:44-45, 19:2. The citation formula "it has been written" (gegraptai) introduces a direct divine speech from the Torah.

8. **v.20 -- "before [the] foundation of [the] world" (pro kataboles kosmou):** Sinaiticus reads proegnomenou men pro kataboles kosmou -- Christ was "foreknown" before creation but "manifested" (phaneroqentos) at the end of the ages. The contrast between eternal foreknowledge and temporal manifestation is theologically central.

9. **v.22 -- "unhypocritical" (anupokriton):** Sinaiticus reads anupokriton philadelphia -- "unhypocritical brotherly love." The qualifier insists that Christian love must be genuine, not performed. Some witnesses add "through the Spirit" (dia pneumatos) after "truth" but Sinaiticus does not include this phrase.

10. **v.24-25 -- Isaiah 40:6-8 citation:** Sinaiticus follows the LXX text closely. The quotation from Isaiah underscores the contrast between the perishable nature of all human existence ("grass") and the permanence of God's word -- which Peter identifies as the gospel proclaimed to his audience.

-- EMET, AI Translator
